titleStmt.title: SII 38.521: original edition by S. Swaminathan
No. 521. (A.R. No. 521 of 1912.) ŪṬṬATTŪR, LALGUDI TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT. On the second gōpura, central shrine Siddharatnēśvara temple.

p.hi: King: Tribhuvanachchakkaravatti Kullōttuṅga Chōḻa III, who was pleased to take Madurai and the crowned head of Pāṇḍya. Year 21: Kumbha, ba. di. 10, Thursday, Viśāka = 24th December 1198 A.D. (Makara and not Kumbha).

summary.p: Built in. Records that (the members of) nāḍu and nagaram of Ūṟṟattūr-nāḍu assembled at the Uttamaśōḻamaṇḍapa (situated) in the temple of Togumāmaṇināyaṉār and ceded the village Veṇmaṇippāḍi in Kīḻvaḷḷuvappāḍi-nāḍu and converted it into a mercantile town (nagaram) called Tāyilunallāpuram, for the merit of Kulōttuṅgaśōḻa Vāṇakōvaraiyar. It is also stated that the nāṭṭār of seventy-nine nāḍus got this transaction engraved on stone. The accountant of the nāḍu figures as the signatory.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
tiripuvaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ śrīmaturaiyum pāṇṭiyaṉ muṭittalaiyuṅ koṇṭaruḷiṉa śrīkulottuṅkacoḻatevaṟku yāṇṭu 2 10 1 vatu kumpanāyaṟṟu Apara
daśamiyum viyāḻakkiḻamaiyum peṟṟa vicākattu nāḷ vaṭakarai Irājarājavaḷanāṭṭu toṟṟattūrRead ūṟṟattūrUṭaiyār tokumāmaṇi nāyaṉār koyil Uttamacoḻar
ttu nāṭum nakaramuṅ kūṭaDo kūṭiIruntu Innāṭṭu kiḻ vaḷḷuvappāṭi nāṭṭu veṇmaṇippāṭiyaik kulottuṅka coḻavāṇa kovaraiyar perāl tāyilunallapuram Enṟu nakaram Eṟa
ṭavum Innakarattu Eṟṟaccurukkañ ceytu ṇṭākil Āka veyiruntu Aṟacceyyakkaṭavatākavum coḻa Ippaṭikku kalveṭṭiviccom Eḻupattoṉpatu nāṭṭom
�ṭṭuk kaṇakku mālavāyuṭaiyār kaṇṇuṭaiyāṉ Aṇukkāṇṭāṉ Eḻuttu U
ṉāṭṭu kaṇarkuṟicci Uṭaiyāṉ tokumāmaṇi taṭuttāṭ koṇṭāṉ Eḻuttu §
Digital edition of SII 38.521 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
